name: Red Sea / Suez Canal
summary: Day 53 of non-mirror status. UKMTO JMIC Advisory Note 031 remains operational reference; Bab el-Mandeb / Gulf of Aden at MODERATE threat — NOT elevated. ZERO confirmed Houthi attack on neutral commercial shipping. Red Sea track held PRISTINE despite Saturday IRGC Hormuz kinetic (tanker + container + cruise-VHF threat). Soufan Center IntelBrief: coordination thesis steadily weaker by time elapsed. The houthis → shipping-tankers edge weight should step below current 9.0 if Houthi kinetic remains absent through Tuesday April 21. Mansour's 'Gulf state directly involved' trigger condition not met by Saturday's Iranian kinetic — Day 53 technically consistent with stated doctrine, coordination thesis weakened not falsified. Container freight FBX ~$2,800/FEU (+40% pre-war, stable). Maersk MECL service remains operational (Danish-flagged through Suez since Jan 2026). Attacks on belligerent-linked (US/UK/Israeli) vessels April 7-9 in scope; neutral shipping untargeted throughout.

signals.content: Houthis entered the war with a missile at Israel on March 28 but have NOT attacked Red Sea shipping (yet). UKMTO and maritime trackers report no new incidents in the Red Sea corridor. However, the Houthi crossing from rhetoric to action materially increases the probability of Red Sea shipping attacks. Michael Knights (Washington Institute) expects incremental escalation: missile at Israel → Red Sea threat → actual shipping attack, spaced 5-10 days apart. Container freight up 3rd consecutive week anticipating disruption. (Sources: UKMTO, IDF, Al Jazeera, Drewry, Maritime Hub)

Day 33: STILL zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping. Attacks remain Israel-targeting only (2 strikes Mar 28-29). However, Bab al-Mandeb is in EFFECTIVE commercial closure due to carrier voluntary withdrawal — Maersk rerouted ME11/MECL to Cape of Good Hope, CMA CGM/Hapag-Lloyd/MSC also rerouted. Bloomberg reports 'divisions within Houthi leadership' partially explaining delay. Houthis told Lloyd's List they see 'no reason to prevent' Yanbu VLCC trade 'at present' — preserving shipping card as leverage. Houthi deputy minister: 'closing Bab al-Mandeb is among our options.' IRGC Tasnim warned of 'new fronts at Red Sea' if ground ops on Iranian islands. EU Operation ASPIDES extended to Feb 2027. UKMTO threat level: CRITICAL. The pattern: threat is generating commercial cost (rerouting, insurance) without expenditure — the threat IS the weapon. (Sources: UKMTO, MARAD, gCaptain, Bloomberg, Lloyd's List, France 24)

Day 35: Still zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping. Pattern holds: rhetoric without maritime action. EU Operation ASPIDES ready for resumed attacks. MARAD Advisory 2026-006 remains active. Effective commercial closure via carrier voluntary withdrawal (Maersk, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, MSC rerouting to Cape). Bloomberg reports Houthi leadership divisions. Iran pushing Houthis to 'prepare for renewed campaign' per European officials (Bloomberg). The non-attack is intelligence: 35 days of threats without shipping action suggests Houthis are being held as a strategic reserve or internal divisions are real. (Sources: MARAD, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, France 24)

Day 38 of war — zero confirmed Houthi Red Sea shipping attacks. Commercial closure via carrier withdrawal continues (Maersk, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, MSC rerouted to Cape of Good Hope). EU ASPIDES task force on station. UKMTO no new advisories. Container freight rising: Asia-Med $3,850/FEU (+15% WoW). The War Zone reports fears Houthis could effectively shut down Bab el-Mandeb, which would 'choke off Saudi oil exports to the east, exacerbating the oil price spike.' The Red Sea remains commercially closed NOT due to military blockade but due to voluntary rerouting and insurance costs — the threat IS the weapon. Despite F-15 shootdown, Trump rhetoric, and deadline extensions, Houthis have not activated the Red Sea shipping card (Day 38). This is the longest Houthi restraint period since their initial 2024 campaign.

DAY 39 — STILL ZERO HOUTHI ATTACKS ON RED SEA SHIPPING despite Houthis joining the first formally announced tripartite cruise missile/drone salvo against Israel April 6. EU ASPIDES task force remains on station. The 'further US escalation' trigger Bloomberg identified has now been crossed multiple times (F-15 shootdown, deadline countdown, 15 US wounded at Ali Al Salem) — yet Houthis are firing AT Israel, not at commercial shipping. The bifurcation between Israel-targeting (active) and Red Sea shipping (untouched) is the most underappreciated escalation/restraint dynamic of the war. Container Freight Asia-Med $3,800/FEU (back from $4,300); Asia-NEU $2,900/FEU; April fuel surcharges $300-500/FEU added.

DAY 40 — ZERO CONFIRMED HOUTHI COMMERCIAL SHIPPING ATTACKS IN RED SEA [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. Day 40 of the war and Houthi commercial shipping attacks remain at ZERO. UKMTO no fresh advisories. Houthis ARE attacking Israel directly (99th wave of Operation True Promise IV April 7) but the Red Sea bifurcation holds. Container freight rates (Freightos FBX) ~$2,287/FEU flat w/w pre-ceasefire — war risk surcharges $1,500-4,000/container. Carrier rerouting (Maersk, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, MSC) won't reverse for 4-8 weeks even on holding ceasefire — vessel schedules need rebalancing. The Lebanon exclusion in the new ceasefire creates a Houthi compliance test: will they obey the bilateral US-Iran cease-fire or use the Lebanon loophole to continue Israeli strikes?

DAY 41 — ZERO CONFIRMED HOUTHI COMMERCIAL SHIPPING ATTACKS [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. The bifurcation holds despite the strongest test conditions of the war: Operation Eternal Darkness (Israel killed 254 in Lebanon April 8) gave Houthis explicit operational pretext under their stated 'we attack to defend Lebanon' framing — and they did not act. NO formal Houthi statement endorsing OR rejecting the bilateral US-Iran ceasefire. The deliberate silence is intelligence: Houthis preserving optionality. The National (Abu Dhabi) characterizes the Houthi posture as a selective 'tollbooth' system rather than blanket compliance. Container freight FBX Asia-USWC $2,400+/FEU (+40% since war start), Asia-USWC +11% this week, Asia-USEC +5% — rates STILL not declining on ceasefire. War-driven rerouting persists; carrier (Maersk, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, MSC) schedules require weeks to rebalance. UKMTO no fresh advisories.

DAY 44 — MAERSK COMPLETED FIRST RED SEA TRANSIT IN TWO YEARS [CONFIRMED — BELLWETHER SIGNAL]. This is the most significant commercial shipping confidence signal since the war began. Maersk had rerouted ME11/MECL services around the Cape of Good Hope since October 2023 — over two years of war-risk avoidance. The single Maersk vessel's Red Sea transit is a material vote of confidence in the durability of the Houthi bifurcation. It is not an operational all-clear (other major carriers — CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, MSC — have not followed yet, and carrier schedules take 4-8 weeks to rebalance), but it is the first time a top-5 container line has re-entered since 2023. UKMTO Update 030 (covering March 1 - April 9) confirms zero attacks, suspicious approaches, or reported incidents in southern Red Sea, Bab al-Mandeb, or adjacent waters. ~57 vessel transits in 24 hours at/near historical normal. Threat level: MODERATE. Houthi posture UPDATED: the last confirmed Houthi strike on Israel was April 6 (UAVs targeting Eilat); post-ceasefire (April 7-11) no confirmed Israel strikes either — possible operational pause aligned with ceasefire window. Al-Masirah framed the silence as 'regroup, not path to peace.' For the trigger point 'Houthis attack commercial shipping' — status STAYS AT 'watching,' but with reduced concern after Maersk transit validates bifurcation. Container freight FBX Asia-USWC ~$2,400/FEU still elevated (+11% WoW, +40% pre-war) — rate normalization lags the operational signal by weeks. (Sources: UKMTO Update 030, SupplyChainBrain, MARAD 2026-006, Times of Israel, Al-Masirah)

UKMTO reported armed boarding attempt April 12 off Hudaydah — NOT attributed to Houthis. Maersk structural return to Red Sea continues. Houthi 'unity of fronts' statement issued but no confirmed Red Sea shipping attacks (Day 46 of zero). Armed boarding bears monitoring but lack of Houthi attribution keeps bifurcation thesis intact.

HOUTHI ATTACKS ON BELLIGERENT-LINKED VESSELS RESUMED IN RED SEA/GULF OF ADEN [CONFIRMED]. April 7: Houthis attacked British-owned Hope Island, Israeli-owned MSC Grace F and MSC Gina, and two US frigates. April 9: Maersk Yorktown (US-flagged) targeted in Gulf of Aden. Bifurcation INTACT for neutral shipping — attacks ONLY on US/UK/Israeli-linked vessels. Maersk MECL (Danish-flagged) still transiting Suez. Bab el-Mandeb closure threatened but NOT enacted despite US blockade meeting stated escalation threshold. Stimson Center: decision 'ultimately set in Tehran.' (Sources: Wikipedia Red Sea crisis, UN SC Resolution 2812, Time, JPost, Stimson Center)

DAY 53 OF NON-MIRROR STATUS — SOUFAN CENTER COORDINATION THESIS STEADILY WEAKER BY TIME [CONFIRMED NON-EVENT]. Zero Houthi kinetic on commercial shipping in Red Sea / Bab-el-Mandeb / Gulf of Aden through Sunday 09:00 IST, despite Saturday April 18 IRGC Hormuz kinetic (tanker + container + cruise-VHF threat) and Sunday SNSC institutional ratification. UKMTO JMIC Advisory Note 031 remains operational reference, MODERATE threat level unchanged. Soufan Center IntelBrief framing: the Iran-Houthi coordination thesis is steadily WEAKER by time elapsed — even if technically consistent with Mansour's 'Gulf state directly involved' doctrine (Saturday condition unmet), a week+ of Iranian escalation without Houthi mirroring erodes the thesis's predictive power. If Houthi kinetic continues absent through Tuesday April 21, the houthis → shipping-tankers edge weight should step below current 9.0. Container freight FBX stable. Maersk MECL structural Red Sea return continues. (Sources: UKMTO JMIC, Soufan Center IntelBrief)

edges.context: Houthi attacks on belligerent-linked vessels resumed April 7 and 9. Neutral shipping untargeted. Bab el-Mandeb closure threatened but not enacted. Bifurcation narrowed to US/UK/Israeli targets only.
Houthi attacks resumed on belligerent-linked vessels. Neutral shipping still transiting. War risk for US/UK/Israeli vessels elevated. ~12% of global trade transits Red Sea/Suez.
If BOTH Hormuz and Red Sea are disrupted simultaneously, there is no safe Gulf export route. This is the worst-case scenario for global energy trade.
Saudi Arabia's Yanbu pipeline exports via Red Sea bypass Hormuz. If Red Sea is also disrupted, this bypass becomes useless. Oil to $130+.

trigger_points.condition: Houthis attack commercial shipping in the Red Sea
trigger_points.mechanism: Double chokepoint activated. Cape of Good Hope only route. Shipping rates double. Oil $130+. India acute energy crisis. Container freight spikes. Global trade disrupted.
